programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: The long cross-examination of the police sergeant continued in the State's against Susan Wright for murder. Prosecutors accused her of not just killing her husband, Jeff, but getting rid of his body and cleaning up the bloody death scene to make it as if nothing happened and Jeff simply disappeared. Next, the lead detective assigned to the case took the stand.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: On the stand was the lead detective assigned to this case. He told jurors that three days before Jeff Wright's body was discovered her wife, Susan, filed a domestic violence complaint against him. Investigators on the Wright property came across red flags when they began processing the backyard where Jeff Wright's remains were found. Items that should be inside the home was found outside.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: On the Stand, the lead detective on the case confirmed that Susan Wright confessed and turned herself into authorities. Next, in a made-for-TV moment a bed and mattress made its way in to the courtroom. Prosecutors re-enacted for the jury the way they were convinced the fatal stabbings took place. They walked jurors through on how Susan tied her husband to the bed and then stabbed him multiple times with two different Knives.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: Prosecutors got very detailed in demonstrating what they believed happened to Jeff Wright right before he was killed. With a bed rolled into the courtroom jurors watched a re-enactment of the fatal stabbing all while the defendant was crying. Next, the lead detective faced cross-examination. He admitted to the jury that cocaine was in Jeff's system at the time he died.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.sub-title: Deal With The Devil - Maggie Ybarra
programme.desc: Nobody knew about suburban dad Michael Hendricks' dark sexual fantasies or his dangerous dalliance with Maggie Ybarra. Behind closed doors, Hendricks and Ybarra nurtured a secret relationship that would turn deadly. In her first-ever interview, Ybarra reveals to host David Scott what it was like to fall under the spell of the Devil Next Door - and how their twisted partnership ended with abusive mind games, a shocking murder, and an innocent life destroyed.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.desc: When flames tore through a Buffalo, New York home in 2018, the investigation revealed a toxic relationship and a hot-tempered arsonist, Frank Bredt, Jr. Now Bredt is talking about his crime from behind bars. In this episode of Interview With a Killer, host David Scott revisits the fire, the evidence, and the unraveling timeline - exposing a jilted lover hidden behind smoke and destruction and giving viewers an unfiltered look into the chilling death of an innocent mother.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.desc: In 2013, the small city of Weatherford, Texas was rocked by the brutal slaying of local son, JD Doss. The murder weapon? A pickaxe. The murderer? His close friend, 24-year-old Nicholas Camfield. In a shocking twist, Camfield not only killed his victim by repeatedly striking him in the head, he also mutilated, scalped, and defiled his body over the course of days. In one of David Scott's most shocking interviews, Camfield gives a glimpse inside his deranged, murderous mind.

programme.desc: In 1996, a series of bank robberies in Texas baffled authorities. But when similar thefts occurred the next year in California, FBI agents linked the crimes and established a modus operandi.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1998, a wealthy woman disappeared from her home in Jackson, Mississippi. The only clue was a mysterious ransom note listing twelve names but no specific instructions. The FBI struggled to find answers in the cryptic message.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Arizona, a shipment of money vanished without a trace, along with the two men hired to guard it. Agents didn't know if the guards were willing participants or victims of an ambush. Suspicions grew that the crime was an inside job.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In October 1987, a Boston police officer responding to a domestic disturbance call was fatally wounded.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the late 1980s, the FBI matched wits with a cunning bank robber known as the Shootist. He had performed more than 50 robberies with military precision and was responsible for the longest string of unsolved bank robberies in FBI history.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Billy Mansfield Jr. moved between Florida and Michigan, targeting vulnerable women and girls. Between 1975 and 1980, Mansfield Jr. murdered at least five victims, burying four of them at his family home in Spring Hill, Florida.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Musician and special needs teacher Jane Longhurst vanished during her day off work after making a plan to go swimming with her friend's boyfriend. Five weeks later, Jane's partially decomposed body was found burning 25 miles from Jane's home.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A dangerous sexual predator murders a young woman, just days after his release from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the killer who took four lives during a chilling two-day killing spree in 2004 after being inspired by horror films.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Joshua Wade had all the hall marks of a killer. We'll never understand the motives behind his random killings.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Denver Simmons is as dangerous as he is ruthless. Incarcerated for murdering a woman and her 14-year-old son, his violence only spiraled further once he was behind bars.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Being behind bars only made Italian-American mobster Freddy Geas even more brutal.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Nearing the end of a lengthy prison sentence, Curtis Watson does the unthinkable: he sexually assaults and murders a prison administrator before escaping and going on the run. A four-day manhunt ensues.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: After shooting two people, Rodney Berget kidnaps and sexually assaults a gas station clerk. Prison struggles to contain him: an escape expert, his final attempt involves the sadistic murder of a corrections officer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look into the case of a sadistic killer who posed as an MI5 agent in order to torture and murder his victim.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Maria's marriage crumbled when Malcolm discovered her bigamy. As he moved on and found new love, Maria's jealousy erupted into dangerous rage, leading to a confrontation that exposed how far she was willing to go.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Randy Kraft was a well-spoken, well-respected computer expert...and a prolific serial killer. Convicted in 1989 of murdering 16 men, he's suspected of having taken the lives of more than 60 victims.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The Bayou Strangler deceived, bound, assaulted and murdered men, as he drifted across South Louisiana.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A convicted killer serving life for manslaughter is found to be guilty of a second murder 32 years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A dangerous sexual predator murders a young woman, just days after his release from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Keith Jesperson, known as the Smiley Face Killer, killed eight women across several states in the 1990s.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look into the case of a sadistic killer who posed as an MI5 agent in order to torture and murder his victim.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Charles Albright's entire life revolved around crime. He graduated from being a small-time crook, into a child-molester and then evolved into a serial killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Arthur Shawcross, known as the 'Genesee River Killer,' was a serial killer who murdered eleven women.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: She became the first woman to be executed by lethal injection after taking six lives. What drove her to commit these callous murders?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Rosemary Anderson is found dead on the side of the road, her boyfriend becomes the prime suspect. He eventually confessed to her murder, but so did another man - serial killer, Eric Edgar Cooke.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Digital enhancement of security camera video shows that what appears to be a casual encounter is actually a forced abduction, leading to murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Joann Katrinak is found dead in a corn field with her son, the obvious suspect is her husband. But insects found on the bodies reveal a vital clue which uncovers a twisted story.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 1995 fire razes the mansion of a Kansas doctor, killing two of her three children; disturbing accounts from an ex-husband lead investigators to believe the doctor set the blaze herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Doctors don't know why a scientist is gravely ill. When tests reveal the cause, it's too late to save him.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When DNA proves that a man who practically admits to a brutal attack is innocent, police wonder why he is willing to take the blame.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her car, the scene had been staged to make murder appear to be suicide. During their investigation, police discovered the victim was involved in a love quadrangle, giving several people a motive to kill her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A mother of two vanishes after a shopping trip. Her body is discovered a month later. Witnesses say they saw the victim being forced into a car by an unknown person.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Forensic computer analysis, blood spatter analysis and a painstaking investigation lead police to a lawyer husband Michael "Mick" Fletcher with a motive to kill his wife since he was having a sexual affair with judge Susan Chrzanowski.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In one of the most brazen crimes of the 20th century, the chairman of the Coors Brewing Company was kidnapped.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two women from the same town were murdered in the same way, police feared a serial killer was on the loose. At first they thought the victims had nothing in common until they found tiny clues linking them to the same man.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bombings are difficult to solve, because the perpetrator isn't usually at the scene, and the evidence goes up in smoke. In this case pieces of plastic the size of grains of sand hold the key to a man's murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim had ingested a massive amount of cyanide. An unlikely clue - a flaw on a mailing envelope - exposed a murderer who was willing to kill innocent people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Nancy Newman and her two daughters are found brutally murdered. The crime scene has no shortage of evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For 15 months, a serial killer was strangling prostitutes in Florida then taunting police by leaving the bodies in plain sight. The only clues were a tire impression and some threads.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An assistant manager of a Florida steakhouse is stabbed to death. It appears to be a robbery gone wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A neighbor watched as a man stabbed his wife, pushed her into the swimming pool and held her head under water.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Women in a Louisiana city live in fear of a rapist who leaves no clues to his identity. But technology and behavioral science combine to give police a new forensic tool.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Russian immigrant is found dead and a strange phone call to his office is the only lead investigators have.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2003, St. Cloud teenager Jason MacLennan returned home after a night out with friends and discovered his father Ken's body lying in a pool of blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While at a real estate conference in St. Louis, a man finds his wife drowned in the bathtub in their hotel room.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is found dead in a ravine, but crucial crime scene evidence had been washed away by severe thunderstorms. Almost 20 years later, an old hat brought the killer to justice.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teen was killed by her parents in what they said was self-defense, but forensic evidence told otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a dedicated, well-respected teacher disappeared, police had to determine if she'd gone on vacation without telling anyone, or if she was the victim of foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of an attractive young woman was found a mile from her abandoned car. Police were especially concerned when they realized the victim had come to them for protection just two weeks earlier.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young woman attends evening church services... then disappears. When her abandoned car is found, the tank is empty and a gas can she kept in her trunk for emergencies is missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man's story of a killing contradicted physical evidence, investigators turned to forensic science.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police charge a mentally unstable man with his wife's murder, but a medical examiner discovers that she died of natural causes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: With no forensic evidence inside a murder scene, investigators were baffled. But they suspected that the victim's dog had witnessed the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Michigan State University grad student disappeared and was presumed dead. With the help of a professor of geological sciences, police hoped to get the "dirt" on her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A bullet-riddled car, a missing driver, and no witnesses, an ambush or a random attack; the clue was something so tiny, it was measured in millionths of a meter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a decomposed body is found stuffed in a barrel, it's easy to presume murder. One of the few clues was an address book found along with the body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspect's former lover comes forward with a tale of murder, mutilation and cremation, but there's no way to test the story's validity until a plant pathologist and a dendrochronologist conduct tests where the cremation supposedly occurred.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A killer tried to incinerate and destroy everything that could link him to his crime. But in doing so, he inadvertently created new forensic evidence, evidence which came to light with a technique never before used in a criminal investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On October 15, 1985, two bomb explosions rocked Salt Lake City and resulted in two deaths. A third explosion occurred the next day. This time, the victim was injured but survived.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1980, Michigan resident Shannon Mohr died in what was reported to be a horseback riding accident by her new husband David Davis. After her death, Shannon's family reported to police their suspicions that Davis seemed far from grief-stricken.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of a young girl was discovered on isolated farmland near Delano, California. She had no ID, but police found mailbox keys in the pocket of her jeans.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An inebriated man makes death threats after being removed from a bar. A deputy investigates a possible stolen vehicle in a grocery store parking lot. A man gives officers an unbelievable story.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy tries to make contact with a parolee who has a history of fighting. Officers respond to an assault at a gas station. A husband allegedly assaults his wife.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers try to clear out several hostile transients who are camping illegally. A deputy describes a mental game he uses to keep him sharp while on patrol. A man refuses to leave a car dealership.
